$schema_note: Each entry drives one integration test against POST /api/v1/screen/public-demo. expected.min_top_confidence sets the floor for the highest-confidence match (engine output is deterministic for fixture data, ±0.05). expected.risk_level matches the bucket derived from confidence. expected.list_ids is the set of source lists that MUST appear among matches.

queries.name: Vladmir Putin
queries.comment: Typo: Vladmir not Vladimir. Should still match via fuzzy/phonetic.

queries.name: Recep Tayyip Erdogan
queries.comment: PEP query: Erdogan should be flagged as PEP. The engine emits some low-confidence phonetic noise vs the sanctions corpus; that is acceptable so long as risk stays out of medium/high.

queries.name: John Smith
queries.comment: Common name with no sanctions or PEP hits. The engine still emits phonetic noise against unrelated entities; risk must not reach high.

queries.name: Vladimir Putin
queries.comment: High threshold should drop most matches; only exact-equivalent survive.

queries.name: Putin
queries.comment: Single-token query; should still match Putin entities via token/fuzzy.

queries.name: Kim Jung-un
queries.comment: Embedding-tier transliteration drift of Kim Jong Un. The in-memory trigram-cosine embedding matcher now binds this to the OFAC alias 'Kim Jung Un'. Phonetic still fires too; both layers are pinned to keep the test honest about which layers carry the drift signal.

queries.name: Bashar al-Asad
queries.comment: Embedding-tier Arabic transliteration drift (single-s) of Bashar al-Assad. The trigram-cosine embedding matcher catches the dropped 's'; Phonetic also fires. Both layers are pinned.

queries.name: Volodymyr Putin
queries.comment: Embedding-tier semantic drift: Ukrainian-style transliteration of Vladimir Putin. Both the embedding layer and Phonetic surface the OFAC alias 'Vladimir Vladimirovich Putin'.

queries.name: Pootin
queries.comment: Embedding-near miss (negative control): phonetic-only spelling of Putin under a 0.75 threshold. The trigram-cosine matcher does not bind 'pootin' to the Putin cluster; the high threshold prunes the noise and the query resolves to clear/low.

queries.name: Владимир Путин
queries.comment: Cyrillic-script query for Vladimir Putin. The public-demo handler transliterates to 'vladimir putin' via expandQuery before the engine runs, then the full cascade (Exact/Fuzzy/Phonetic/Token/Embedding) fires against the OFAC/EU/UK Putin entries.

queries.name: بشار الأسد
queries.comment: Arabic-script query for Bashar al-Assad. TransliterateArabic produces an approximate Latin form ('bshar al-asd'); the embedding + phonetic layers catch the drift against the OFAC entry.

queries.name: Сергей Лавров
queries.comment: Cyrillic-script transliteration variant of Sergey Lavrov. expandQuery yields 'sergey lavrov', and the cascade surfaces the OFAC entry.

queries.name: Volodymyr Putyn
queries.comment: Mixed-script-style cross-transliteration: Ukrainian first name plus a non-standard Latin 'Putyn' spelling. Embedding (trigram-cosine) surfaces the Putin cluster despite no clean Phonetic/Exact bind.
